headline: 2'+ choppy but workable for a longboard. Larger swell filling in tonight!
text: 8:15 AM outgoing tide check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2 foot plus breaking nearshore with ENE onshore winds chopping it up. Workable for a longboard if you're itching to paddle out. The good news: reports are showing a larger period swell filling in this evening with better winds coming tomorrow and Thursday. Could be a fun midweek run. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 28, 2026 | 2ft+ Choppy, Swell Building Tonight
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 28: 2ft+ breaking nearshore with ENE onshore chop at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Longboard workable. Larger period swell filling in tonight with better winds Wednesday and Thursday.

headline: 2-3' choppy NE onshore — barely enough to make a board go. Tomorrow may be surfable.
text: 5:25 PM high tide check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot — 2-3 foot and choppy with NE onshore winds making it bumpy and barely enough to push a board. Not the session you're looking for today. Good news is tomorrow's forecast is looking more surfable so we'll be back at first light to check. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 27, 2026 PM | 2-3ft Choppy NE Onshore
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 27 afternoon: 2-3ft choppy surf with NE onshore winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Bumpy conditions, barely surfable. Better surf expected Tuesday.

headline: Cocoa Beach totally flat — 1' breaking into shore. Skimboard at high tide!
text: 7 AM high tide check at 31st Street — totally flat with 1 foot breaking into shore. Maybe something for a skimboard at high tide report time but that's about it. Looks like a little bump in the swell later this week. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 27, 2026 | Flat 1ft, High Tide Skimboard
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 27: flat 1ft breaking into shore at Picnic Tables, 31st Street South Cocoa Beach. Skimboard conditions at high tide. Swell bump coming later this week.

headline: 1' nearshore with a SSW bump on the tiny line. Beautiful beach day!
text: Middle tide report from 31st Street — 1 foot breaking nearshore with SSW winds putting a bump on the tiny line. Not much to surf but an absolutely beautiful beach day at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. Grab a towel and enjoy it.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Sunday April 26, 2026 | 1ft Nearshore, SSW Wind
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Sunday April 26: 1ft breaking nearshore at Picnic Tables, 31st Street. SSW winds, middle tide, beautiful beach day at South Cocoa Beach.

headline: 1' onshore mush — ESE winds killing it at Picnic Tables. Check back tomorrow morning.
text: Afternoon check at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot and conditions aren't doing us any favors right now. 1 foot breaking nearshore with onshore ESE winds making it messy and blown out. Not worth paddling out. We'll be back at first light tomorrow when winds typically go offshore — morning glass is always the move. Stay tuned.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Saturday April 25, 2026 PM | Onshore ESE, 1ft Nearshore
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Saturday April 25 afternoon: 1ft nearshore with onshore ESE winds at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Conditions poor. Checking again Sunday morning.

headline: 1' and very small breaking against shore. Great day to teach the kids!
text: Very small breaking against shore at 7 am. 1 foot and barely rideable but a beautiful morning at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. If you've got groms that want to learn, today's the day — warm water, no current, and nothing to be afraid of. Athena approves.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Saturday April 25, 2026 | 1ft Nearshore, Beginner Friendly
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Saturday April 25: 1ft breaking against shore at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Beginner friendly, warm water, great day for kids.

headline: 1-2' clean with fun sets. Aloha Friday — grab your longboard!
text: 8 AM low tide check — 1-2' breaking nearshore but some very fun sets coming through. West wind keeping it glassy and clean at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. Beautiful Aloha Friday out there. Athena says get out there with your big boards.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Friday April 24, 2026 | 1-2ft Clean, West Wind Glass
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report for Friday April 24: 1-2 foot clean waves at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Offshore west winds, fun sets, great longboard conditions.

headline: 2-3' and fun for a longboard. Winds finally down!
text: Lowtide report time, wind has come down considerably but so has the surf. It's 2-3' and probably fun for a longboard. Beautiful sunrise this morning at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot!
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Thursday April 23, 2026 | 2-3ft, Longboard Fun
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Thursday April 23: 2-3ft waves at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Winds down, fun longboard conditions, beautiful sunrise.

headline: 2-3'+ with easing winds but still poor form at lowtide.
text: Wind has finally eased up some, but is still onshore with gusts over 12 kts. Surf has come down some as well 2-3'+ with still poor form. Lowtide at report time wasn't too appealing. Cocoa Beach surf conditions remain below average.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Wednesday April 22, 2026 | 2-3ft+ Onshore, Poor Form
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Wednesday April 22: 2-3ft+ waves at Picnic Tables with onshore winds over 12 knots. Poor form at low tide, below average conditions.

headline: 3-5' washing machine continues. ENE winds keeping quality low.
text: Choppy washing machine conditions continue with erosion visible on the beach. 3-5' at lowtide but persistent ENE winds have the quality low across Cocoa Beach's best surf spot.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 21, 2026 PM | 3-5ft Choppy, ENE Wind
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 21 afternoon: 3-5ft choppy washing machine conditions at Picnic Tables. ENE winds keeping quality low, beach erosion visible.

headline: 3-5' but a choppy, wind-blown mess. Stay home and walk the dog.
text: Extremely windy, low tide incoming and a chopped up mess at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. Surfable, but doesn't look like much fun. Stay home and walk the dog.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Tuesday April 21, 2026 AM | 3-5ft Blown Out
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Tuesday April 21 morning: 3-5ft blown out surf at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. Extremely windy, choppy mess at low tide.

headline: 3-4'+ with 20kt+ wind gusts. Stay home and walk the dog.
text: Extremely windy, low tide incoming and a chopped up mess at Cocoa Beach's best surf spot. Surfable, but doesn't look like much fun. Stay home and walk the dog.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 20, 2026 PM | 3-4ft+, 20kt Gusts
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 20 afternoon: 3-4ft+ waves at Picnic Tables with 20+ knot wind gusts. Poor conditions, blown out surf.

headline: 2-3'+ wind driven victory at sea conditions.
text: A fast drift and maybe a surfable wave at high tide incoming at report time. Feels like a Monday, happy high holiday to those who celebrate. Cocoa Beach's best surf spot doing its thing.
seoTitle: Cocoa Beach Surf Report — Monday April 20, 2026 AM | 2-3ft+ Wind Swell
seoDescription: Cocoa Beach surf report Monday April 20 morning: 2-3ft+ wind driven surf at Picnic Tables, South Cocoa Beach. High tide incoming, fast drift conditions.
